l1: Question 1 — When did the iSCSI connectivity issue first appear?
Question 2 — Is the problem affecting all targets or a specific one?
Question 3 — Has the iSCSI initiator been configured recently?
Question 4 — Is the issue intermittent or persistent?
Question 5 — Are multiple servers affected or only one?
l2: Advanced question 1 — Are there network changes such as VLAN or firewall updates?
Advanced question 2 — Is multipath I/O (MPIO) configured and functioning correctly?
Advanced question 3 — Are authentication methods (CHAP, mutual CHAP) in use?
Advanced question 4 — Is the iSCSI target part of clustered storage or SAN?
Advanced question 5 — Are there performance drops before disconnections?

l1: Command or question 1 — Open iSCSI Initiator and check target status
Command or question 2 — Run PowerShell Get-IscsiSession to confirm active sessions
Command or question 3 — Verify connectivity with ping to target IP
Command or question 4 — Check Event Viewer for iSCSI-related warnings
Command or question 5 — Run iscsicli ListTargets to list configured targets
l2: Advanced command 1 — Run Get-IscsiConnection to check detailed session info
Advanced command 2 — Use iscsicli QLoginTarget to query login status
Advanced command 3 — Run netstat -an to confirm TCP port 3260 connectivity
Advanced command 4 — Check MPIO configuration with Get-MSDSMGlobalDefaultLoadBalancePolicy
Advanced command 5 — Review firewall rules for iSCSI traffic allowance

l1: Resolution step 1 — Restart iSCSI Initiator service
Resolution step 2 — Verify target IP and DNS resolution
Resolution step 3 — Re-enter target portal details in iSCSI Initiator
Resolution step 4 — Ensure firewall allows TCP port 3260
Resolution step 5 — Test basic connectivity with ping and tracert
l2: Advanced fix 1 — Reconfigure CHAP authentication if credentials changed
Advanced fix 2 — Update NIC drivers and firmware for iSCSI traffic
Advanced fix 3 — Configure MPIO properly for redundancy and load balancing
Advanced fix 4 — Run iscsicli RefreshTargetPortal to refresh stale connections
Advanced fix 5 — Escalate to SAN vendor if target-side issues suspected

l1: Export log X — Save Event Viewer iSCSI logs
Collect trace Y — Run Get-IscsiSession and export results
Run command Z — Execute iscsicli ListTargets and save output
Step 4 — Capture screenshot of iSCSI Initiator target list
Step 5 — Collect OS build and patch level
l2: Advanced collection 1 — Export Get-IscsiConnection detailed session report
Advanced collection 2 — Collect MPIO configuration and policies
Advanced collection 3 — Gather performance monitor counters for iSCSI traffic
Advanced collection 4 — Export firewall rules related to TCP port 3260
Advanced collection 5 — Collect SAN-side logs for target events

iscsi_disconnect.title: iSCSI target disconnects or fails to connect

steps.action: Run Get-IscsiSession to confirm session state
steps.expect: Session shows disconnected or error state

steps.action: Ping target IP and check firewall rules
Restart iSCSI Initiator service and re-login to target
Run Get-IscsiConnection and check Event Viewer
steps.expect: Healthy session with no new errors
